text:
Department of Neurology

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025

Dr Amelia Grant
South Thames Neurology Centre
2nd Floor, Riverside Wing, St Martin’s Hospital, London SE11 3PA

Dear Dr Amelia Grant,

Michael Turner, DOB: 14-03-1991, Hospital No: H392771 NHS No. 9472158036
Flat 5, 28 Willow Street, London, EC2A 4BH

Diagnoses:

Recurrent unprovoked seizures (classification not established)

Current medication:

Levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily
Rescue: Buccal midazolam 10 mg as needed for prolonged events

Plan:

- Increase Levetiracetam to 750 mg twice daily
- Continue safety and lifestyle measures; maintain seizure diary app use
- MRI brain with epilepsy protocol and routine EEG
- Bloods: FBC, U&E, LFTs, Vitamin D
- DVLA guidance reinforced; no driving
- Follow-up in 8–10 weeks or sooner if events escalate

I reviewed Michael Turner in clinic today. He shared detailed entries from his seizure diary app, including time-stamped logs, associated symptoms, and potential triggers. Over the last fortnight, he has logged about 3 or 5 seizures in the last two weeks, with events typically occurring in the early morning shortly after waking. The diary entries describe brief loss of awareness with bilateral limb stiffening and jerking lasting 60–90 seconds, followed by confusion and headache for up to 30 minutes. No clear precipitant is identified, although he noted reduced sleep and higher work-related stress preceding two of the events.

There is no tongue biting or urinary incontinence reported in recent episodes. He has sustained minor bruising from one fall without head injury. No focal neurological symptoms between events. He denies alcohol or recreational drug use. Family history is non-contributory. He commenced Levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily three months ago with good tolerability (mild irritability noted in the diary during the first two weeks which has since settled). Despite adherence, the recent increase in frequency captured on the app suggests suboptimal control.

Examination today was unremarkable with normal cardiorespiratory and neurological findings. Baseline blood tests from June were within normal limits. To progress evaluation, I have arranged an MRI brain with an epilepsy protocol and a routine EEG to look for supportive features, and I have asked him to continue using the diary app and to upload videos if safe for bystanders to capture. We discussed safety: supervised bathing, avoiding heights/open water, and using a microwave rather than open hob when alone. DVLA regulations were reviewed; he understands he must not drive and has agreed to notify the DVLA if he has not already done so.

Given the recent pattern recorded in the app, I have recommended increasing Levetiracetam to 750 mg twice daily. He has a supply and understands a simple up-titration over three days. Buccal midazolam remains for rescue in prolonged events. I have provided our urgent advice line details should the frequency increase further or if he experiences any new features such as prolonged clusters, injuries, or atypical focal symptoms.

I will review him in 8–10 weeks with the imaging and EEG results and will reassess treatment options based on response and investigations.

With best wishes,

Yours sincerely,

entities.text: Over the last fortnight, he has logged about 3 or 5 seizures in the last two weeks
about 3 or 5 seizures in the last two weeks

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: "Over the last fortnight, he has logged about 3 or 5 seizures in the last two weeks." This provides a numeric range for a defined time period (two weeks = fortnight). According to the rules, use explicit numeric expressions and normalize to allowed format. The range is 3 to 5 over 2 weeks, which maps directly to "3 to 5 per 2 week". No other conflicting frequencies are provided, and he is not seizure-free. Therefore, the highest/only frequency is 3 to 5 per 2 week.
seizure_frequency_number: Over the last fortnight, he has logged about 3 or 5 seizures in the last two weeks
reference: about 3 or 5 seizures in the last two weeks
